name: Russian Potato Casserole with Caramelized Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the 5 tablespoons butter in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the olive oil and heat.
2. Add the thinly sliced onions to the pan and stir to coat with the butter. Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions turn deep brown (about an hour).
3. Sprinkle onions with sugar and continue cooking for a few more minutes.
4. Boil potatoes in enough chicken stock to cover in a large pot over high heat until slightly underdone (about 15-20 minutes). Drain potatoes completely and reserve some stock.
5. Mash the potatoes, adding butter to taste.
6. Stir beaten eggs into one cup of sour cream.
7. Add this egg mixture to the mashed potatoes, stirring quickly to distribute evenly.
8. Stir in heavy cream, dill weed, and enough reserved chicken stock to achieve a loose potato consistency.
9. Transfer half the potato mixture to a greased casserole dish. Spread caramelized onions evenly over the potatoes.
10. Cover with remaining potato mixture and top with 2 cups sour cream.
11. Bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
